goal: Define the auth and persistence boundary before the mock Builder Adapter route gets any approval, storage, or live integration behavior.
claims: Auth and persistence are explicitly separated into future contracts.
The next safe auth step is mock-only and must not use real tokens or secrets.
Persistence remains prohibited until a storage contract defines schema, retention, and evidence rules.
assumptions: The current mock HTTP route is reviewable without auth or persistence.
A governance boundary should come before any auth or storage code.
No runtime, package, tool, or example change is required.
dod: The document separates auth, persistence, live Builder integration, and local mock behavior.
The document defines allowed next steps and blocked steps.
stop_conditions: Auth or persistence implementation becomes necessary
Secret, database, or live Builder configuration becomes necessary
prior_task_findings: BP-014 added a local review-suite command and identified auth, persistence, deployment, and live Builder wiring as requiring fresh contracts.
